description:
Your university high-power rocketry team is trying to break the sound barrier (Mach 1.0) with a two-stage rocket. The baseline design is located at /home/ga/Documents/rockets/transonic_rocket.ork. Currently, all external components have a 'Rough' surface finish, and all fin sets have a 'Square' cross-section, which causes excessive aerodynamic drag at transonic speeds.

Your task:
1. Open the design in OpenRocket and run the existing simulation to see the baseline Max Velocity and Max Altitude.
2. Change the cross-section of ALL fin sets (on both the booster and sustainer) to 'Airfoil'.
3. Change the Surface Finish of ALL external structural components (Nose Cone, Body Tubes, Transitions, and Fin Sets) to 'Polished'.
4. Re-run the simulation to verify the velocity increase.
5. Save the optimized rocket design as /home/ga/Documents/rockets/transonic_rocket_optimized.ork.
6. Write a summary report to /home/ga/Documents/exports/aerodynamic_report.txt comparing the before and after maximum velocity and altitude.
